svg.text: Click a row to pin details · Click ▾/▸ to expand/collapse · Ctrl+F to search
select straight_join testdb.t1.id AS id,testdb.t1.a AS a,testdb.t1.b AS b,testdb.t1.c AS c,testdb.t1.d AS d
Each row = one plan operation. Indentation shows parent→child (outer→inner). Self = time in this op only; Total = including children.
Click ▾/▸ to collapse/expand a subtree. Click a row to pin its details. Ctrl+F to search.
Orange rows ⚠ have a warning in the Query Analysis panel below.
batched_key_access=on — Collects keys from the outer table into the join buffer, sorts them, then uses MRR to fetch inner rows in (mostly) index order — cuts random I/O on large
join_cache_bka=on — MariaDB variant of batched_key_access: inner rows are fetched in batches keyed from the join buffer, using MRR to reorder I/O.
mrr=on — Multi-Range Read: sorts row IDs before fetching rows so InnoDB reads pages in roughly primary-key order, turning random I/O into sequential I/O on range scans.
• Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
• Full scan on t2 with filter on (y, x)
CREATE INDEX idx_t2_y_x ON t2 (y, x);
